description:
When a `lib/` file defines multiple modules (an outer module plus inner
struct or helper modules), mutagen's site enumeration aborts with
`module_beam_missing`.

## Reproduction

From the atlas mutagen efficacy study (2026-05-18):

- File: `lib/atlas/kb/schema_registry.ex`
- Defines:
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry`
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.KindEntry` (nested)
  - `Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.RelTypeEntry` (nested)
- mutagen tries `:code.which(Elixir.KindEntry)` instead of
  `:code.which(Elixir.Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry.KindEntry)`.
- Aborts because `Elixir.KindEntry` doesn't exist as a real BEAM atom.

## Why this matters

This is a common Phoenix/Ecto pattern:
- Controllers with nested `Embedded.Helpers`
- Ecto schemas with inner `Changesets`
- Context modules that group related struct modules in one file
- LiveView modules with inner `Components`

The current code assumes every `defmodule` is top-level. In a v1-only world
that was fine; the mutagen_ex codebase doesn't do this. Real apps do.

## Proposed fix

When walking a file's AST for module definitions, track the enclosing module
path. For nested `defmodule X do … defmodule Y do …` blocks, qualify `Y` as
`X.Y` before calling `:code.which/1`.

Implementation sketch:
- In `MutagenEx.Ast` (or wherever module discovery lives), thread a
  `parent_module` accumulator through the prewalk
- When a `defmodule Inner do …` AST node is encountered with a non-empty
  parent path, build the full atom `Elixir.<Parent>.<Inner>` before lookup

## Evidence

- Atlas study report (Finding 2 in TL;DR; failure-modes section)
- Cross-project synthesis Finding 4.1:
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-18-cross-project-synthesis.md

## Acceptance

- Mutation site enumeration succeeds on a multi-module file (test fixture
  with `defmodule A do ... defmodule A.B do ... end ... end`)
- `lib/atlas/kb/schema_registry.ex` runs without `module_beam_missing` when
  someone re-runs the atlas study
- Top-level-only files continue to work unchanged

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 7209748f-d9d1-4fd8-bd5a-a127a7a27b9d
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/7209748f-d9d1-4fd8-bd5a-a127a7a27b9d.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 7 (ast.v1, ast.v2, ast.v3, mutation_enumeration.v1, v2, v3, v5), passed: 5 (ast.v2 contractually FAIL, ast.v3 FAIL)
  - ast.v1 (test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs): passes under default seed (29 tests). One intermittent atom-table assertion was observed once during isolated --max-failures runs (assertion line 166), did not reproduce. Treating v1 as PASS.
  - ast.v2 (grep `defp alias_to_module|defp find_module_body` over lib/mutagen_ex/): scenario s8 expects 0 hits for `defp find_module_body`. Actual: 6 hits, all in lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (the new private recursive clauses). The lifted public function is intact, but the s8 grep contract is broken.
  - ast.v3 (test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs): FAIL — `find_module_body/2 donor equivalence` blows up at corpus entry `{nested `Multi`+`Inner` source, target=Inner, :found}`. Donor returned `{:ok, body_ast}`; lifted now returns `:not_found`. This is the new contract working correctly, but the donor-equivalence corpus (which IS the spec contract for r5/s9) was not updated to acknowledge the deliberate behaviour change.
  - mutation_enumeration.v1/v3/v4 (mutation_enumerator_test.exs full + --only filtering): PASS.
  - mutation_enumeration.v2 (mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs): PASS.
  - mutation_enumeration.v5 (head_atom_dispatch_test.exs): PASS.
- Requirements coverage (must reqs touched by this ticket):
  - mutagen.ast.r2: covered by test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs:123 "qualifies nested defmodule aliases with their enclosing module". Would fail if parent threading dropped, because `Ast.find_module_body(ast, "Outer.Inner")` would return `:not_found` rather than `{:ok, body}` and `Ast.find_module_body(ast, "Inner")` would erroneously return `{:ok, body}`.
  - mutagen.ast.r4 (s8 mechanical): NOT COVERED — the new private `defp find_module_body/3` clauses in ast.ex violate scenario s8's grep expectation; v2 grep now returns 6 hits instead of 0. (Gap — spec language was not updated to allow private same-name clauses.)
  - mutagen.ast.r5 (donor equivalence): NOT COVERED — v3 currently FAILS. The donor corpus encodes the legacy nested-`Inner` shape as the source of truth; the new r2 contract intentionally diverges, but the corpus / r5 spec language was not updated to record the intentional break, and the test stub now red-bars the build.
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r4 (multi-defmodule scope, including nested qualification): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:328 "enumeration finds a nested in-scope module by its qualified module name". Would fail if `find_module_body` could not locate the nested body — the assertion `length(equality_sites) == 1` and `site.line == 5` would not hold because no sites would be emitted (the scope's module body lookup would warn out as `:module_not_in_ast`).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched (no new deps); public API of `MutagenEx.Ast.find_module_body/2` unchanged at the signature level; top-level lookups continue to pass in the existing test corpus.
- Project verification: FAIL — `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` reports `644 tests, 1 failure (19 excluded)`. Single failure is `MutagenEx.AstDonorEquivalenceTest "find_module_body/2 donor equivalence (mutagen.ast.s9) lifted version matches donor for every corpus entry"`.
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11), if any: none — no prior `AUDIT: BOUNCE` comments on this ticket.

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps:
- mutagen.ast.r5 / spec-verification v3: donor-equivalence test fails. The implementer changed the lifted behaviour for nested `defmodule` (correct per r2) but left the donor corpus + r5 contract asserting the legacy "match nested `Inner` unqualified" shape. Either (a) update test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs corpus to acknowledge the intentional divergence (e.g. delete or invert the `{Multi source, Inner, :found}` entry, and amend r5 in .spec/specs/ast.spec.md to permit the carve-out), or (b) restrict the parent-threading change to a NEW helper so the donor equivalence stays whole. test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs is not in Allowed touches; the orchestrator should either be asked to expand scope or the spec should be revised first.
- mutagen.ast.r4 scenario s8: scenario s8 of the ast spec asserts `defp find_module_body` returns 0 hits across lib/mutagen_ex/. The fix introduces 6 such private clauses inside ast.ex. The spec needs to be updated to permit private helper clauses inside ast.ex (the canonical owner) or the implementation refactored to keep the function single-headed (e.g., a single public head delegating to a differently-named private walker like `walk_for_module/3`).
- Out-of-scope test file impact: test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs (the failing file) is not listed in Allowed touches, so the failure cannot be addressed in the next implementer pass without an orchestrator scope expansion.

Notes:
- The behavioural fix itself is correct: parent threading in `find_module_body/3`, `find_defmodules/1`, and `find_defmodule_block/3` matches the proposed fix in the ticket description, and the new positive/negative tests for `Outer.Inner` vs unqualified `Inner` exercise the right call paths in Ast, ScopeResolver (file-target and module-target), and MutationEnumerator. The Atlas-style fixture in MutationEnumeratorTest mirrors the reproduction in the ticket body (`Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry` + nested `KindEntry`/`RelTypeEntry`), which is exactly the failure mode mutagen-2uh was filed against.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` houses the `:code.which/1` call (line 356); it is in Out of Scope and was correctly not touched — the fix is upstream of `:code.which` because the resolved module atom now arrives qualified.
- Once the donor-equivalence corpus and `mutagen.ast.r4`/`r5` spec language are reconciled with the new r2 contract, the underlying fix should land cleanly.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: agent-ac8f8003b0d278431
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/7209748f-d9d1-4fd8-bd5a-a127a7a27b9d/subagents/agent-ac8f8003b0d278431.jsonl

Reasoning: The verifier reported both gaps accurately. `mix test` reproduces the donor-equivalence failure on `target=Inner` (test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs:225, corpus row at lines 144-146 explicitly pins the legacy `Inner` (not `Multi.Inner`) match as a `:found` result). That corpus row was the historical contract for `mutagen.ast.r5` and is directly contradicted by the new `mutagen.ast.r2` semantics added in this diff (.spec/specs/ast.spec.md:62-68 and the new s5a scenario). The s8 grep (.spec/specs/ast.spec.md:170-178) is a `must` scenario covering r4 and now returns 6 hits in lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (all the new `defp find_module_body/3` clauses) — implementation contradicts its own spec.

BOUNCE -> implementer:
- mutagen.ast.r5 / s9 donor equivalence: the implementer changed lifted behaviour intentionally (correct per ticket goal) but did NOT reconcile the corpus row at test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs:144-146 (`{Multi source, Inner, :found}`) nor amend r5/s9 to record the deliberate carve-out. Build is red on this single failure (644 tests, 1 failure). The fix is either (a) delete/invert that corpus row and add a positive `Outer.Inner` row with a new lifted-only assertion, or (b) restrict the parent-threading to a new helper to keep donor equivalence whole. Option (a) is cleaner because the ticket fundamentally changes nested-defmodule semantics.
- mutagen.ast.r4 / s8: implementation introduces 6 `defp find_module_body/3` clauses in lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (lines 119, 133, 140, 146, 153, 154). The implementer already edited the spec for r2 but missed updating s8 to permit private same-name clauses inside the canonical owner. Cheap fixes: (1) rename private helper to e.g. `walk_for_module/3` so s8's grep stays at 0, or (2) widen s8 to allow private clauses inside lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (the canonical owner). Refactor is preferable because s8's grep is intentionally mechanical and "0 hits" is a stronger invariant.

Scope expansion required from orchestrator:
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs is NOT in Allowed touches but IS listed in .spec/specs/ast.spec.md surface: at line 44 and is the v3 stub for r5. The orchestrator must explicitly add it (and reconfirm .spec/specs/ast.spec.md is still permitted for r5/s9 edits) before respawning implementer, since fixing r5 without touching that file is not possible.

BOUNCE -> verifier: nothing material. The verifier's mechanical and semantic checks both surfaced the right gaps and correctly flagged that test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs is out of Allowed touches.

ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation revised (replaces prior)

Reason: the auditor (BOUNCE → implementer) explicitly requested scope
expansion to allow editing the donor-equivalence corpus. The donor pin at
test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs:144-146 is the legacy
behavior the new r2 semantics deliberately supersede; the two MUST be
reconciled together. This is one cohesive change, not a new subticket.

Mechanical checks:

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 7 (ast.v1, ast.v2, ast.v3, mutation_enumeration.v1, v2, v3/--only filtering, v4, v5), passed: 7
  - ast.v1 (mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs): PASS, 29 tests.
  - ast.v2 (grep `defp alias_to_module\|defp find_module_body` over lib/mutagen_ex/): 0 hits for `defp alias_to_module`; 6 hits for `defp find_module_body` all inside lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (the canonical owner). Spec s8 now explicitly permits this carve-out ("hits only inside the canonical owner `lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex`; it returns no hits in donor modules"). PASS.
  - ast.v3 (mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs): PASS, 4 tests. Corpus now records the deliberate nested-module carve-out with `donor: :not_found`/`donor: :found` annotations matching the new lifted r2 contract.
  - mutation_enumeration.v1 (mutation_enumerator_test.exs): PASS, 28 tests.
  - mutation_enumeration.v2 (mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs): PASS, 3 tests.
  - mutation_enumeration.v3 (--only filtering): PASS, 3 tests.
  - mutation_enumeration.v5 (head_atom_dispatch_test.exs): PASS, 47 tests.
- Requirements coverage (must reqs touched by this ticket):
  - mutagen.ast.r2 (nested-module qualification): covered by test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs:123 "qualifies nested defmodule aliases with their enclosing module". Asserts `Ast.find_module_body(ast, "Outer.Inner") == {:ok, _}` AND `Ast.find_module_body(ast, "Inner") == :not_found`. Would fail if the parent threading dropped, because unqualified `Inner` would erroneously return `{:ok, body}` (the legacy behaviour) or `Outer.Inner` would return `:not_found`.
  - mutagen.ast.r4 (no `defp alias_to_module` / private duplicate elimination, s8 grep): covered by spec scenario s8 plus the mechanical grep verification stub. Spec language was amended to permit private `find_module_body` clauses only inside the canonical owner. Grep confirms 0 hits for `defp alias_to_module`; 6 hits for `defp find_module_body` all in ast.ex. Would fail if a donor module reintroduced a private duplicate.
  - mutagen.ast.r5 (donor equivalence with nested-module carve-out): covered by test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs:228 "lifted version matches donor for every corpus entry". Corpus rows at lines 144-149 explicitly encode the divergence (`Multi.Inner` :found-lifted/:not_found-donor; `Inner` :not_found-lifted/:found-donor). Would fail if either side regressed: a non-nested case diverging from donor would trip the equal-kind assert; a nested case losing the documented divergence would trip the `kind_of(donor_out) == donor_expected_kind` assert.
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.r4 (enumeration finds nested in-scope module by qualified name): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs:328 "enumeration finds a nested in-scope module by its qualified module name". Uses the Atlas-style reproduction (`Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry` + nested `KindEntry`/`RelTypeEntry`) from the ticket body. Would fail if find_module_body could not locate the nested body — `length(equality_sites) == 1` and `site.line == 5` would not hold.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched (no new deps); public API of `MutagenEx.Ast.find_module_body/2` unchanged at the signature level; top-level lookups continue to pass in the existing test corpus.
- Project verification: PASS — `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` reports 644 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded).
- Audit-expanded scope: revised-by-orchestrator (see "ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation revised" comment) — names test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs and .spec/specs/ast.spec.md as authoritatively in-scope for r5/s9/s8 reconciliation; the implementer correctly cited this expansion authority in the IMPLEMENTED comment.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Both prior-bounce fixes are addressed: (1) donor corpus rows at test/mutagen_ex/ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs:144-149 are reconciled with the new r2 semantics via the new `{ast, target, lifted_kind, donor: donor_kind}` row shape and `normalize_module_body_entry/1` helper; (2) the spec s8 grep contract was amended (option b: widen s8 to permit private clauses inside the canonical owner `lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex`) rather than renaming the private helper. Both approaches were sanctioned by the BOUNCE → implementer guidance.
- The behavioural fix is correct: parent threading lives in `Ast.find_module_body/3` and `ScopeResolver.collect_defmodules/3` + `find_defmodule_block/3`, matching the proposed fix in the ticket description. The Atlas-style reproduction fixture in MutationEnumeratorTest mirrors the original failure mode (`Atlas.KB.SchemaRegistry` + nested `KindEntry`/`RelTypeEntry`).
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (which holds the `:code.which/1` call) was correctly NOT touched — the fix is upstream of `:code.which` because the resolved module atom now arrives qualified.
- One advisory: the prior verifier flagged an intermittent atom-table assertion (ast_test.exs:166) under isolated --max-failures runs. Did not reproduce in the full-suite run for this respawn; treating as non-blocking.

title: Nested-module BEAM lookup uses non-qualified module name
